_comment: Curated seed for a BLOCKED / WHAT-TO-TRY-NEXT section, same pattern as occupations.json.
WHY THIS EXISTS: TRACKER.md counts what is missing. It cannot tell you whether a gap is a
five-minute lookup or a person who has vanished. Twice now a 'one missing census' has eaten an
hour and ended in nothing attached. This file carries what was ALREADY TRIED and what to try
NEXT, so no one re-runs a failed search or re-derives a dead end.
RULE: 'tried' is a record of searches that FAILED. Nothing here is evidence and nothing here
may be attached to the tree. If a lead comes good it moves to the journal and the tree, and
Each entry ticks itself off when the named gap closes in an export - same mechanism as §7.

blocked.name: William Ingleby (Thomas b.1812's father - PLACEHOLDER)
blocked.gap: identity - and it gates 34 disconnected people
tried: Mapped section 6 in full: the 72 disconnected people are 22 ISLANDS, not one problem. Island 2 is 34 people - the whole upper Ingleby line plus the Abbotts - and it hangs off THIS ONE PERSON
blocked.found_instead: THE JOIN POINT IS EXACT. Main tree: Thomas Ingleby I352793559056 b.abt 1812 sits in family F76 whose HUSB is William Ingleby I352794603636 - a placeholder with NO dates and NO sources. Island 2's apex is William Ingleby I352551798670 - b.1794, bapt 19 Sep 1794 Pateley Bridge, 15+ sources, m. Ann Corker 6 Oct 1825 Ripon Cathedral, censuses Bishopside/Fellbeck, d. Mar 1872 bur. Dacre - and behind him George Ingleby b.1760 x Dorothy Hammon, John Ingleby b.1741 x Sarah Kirby, then ANTHONY INGLEBY at the top.
next: ⛔ DO NOT MERGE THE TWO WILLIAMS, however tempting a 34-person payoff is. THE DATES DO NOT WORK: William b.1794 married in 1825, but Thomas was born abt 1812 - thirteen years earlier, when William was 18 - and Thomas is not among F27's six children (Rebecca 1835, George 1837, Robert 1839, David 1842, John 1842, Eliza 1846)
GEOGRAPHY IS CLOSE BUT NOT THE SAME - William b.1794 is Nidderdale (Pateley Bridge, Bishopside, Fellbeck, Dacre); Thomas is 'of Mickley', Kirkby Malzeard / Eavestone / Grewelthorpe

⭐ AND WE HAVE MADE THIS EXACT MISTAKE ALREADY - Thomas was once mis-grafted onto a JOHN Ingleby, 'a REAL but WRONG man', and it took a full untangle to remove him. Grafting him onto a WILLIAM on a matching forename plus a convenient payoff is the same error with a different first name. A merge is IRREVERSIBLE and retires an ID you cannot choose

WHAT IS ACTUALLY NEEDED: Thomas Ingleby's BAPTISM (abt 1812, Mickley/Kirkby Malzeard/Grewelthorpe area) or his MARRIAGE record, either of which should name his father. Kirkby Malzeard and Ripon registers are the target - see the grewelthorpe.org.uk lead

⚠️ NOTE FOR WHOEVER WORKS SECTION 6 - ISLAND 3 (John Ingleby + Mary + Mary Ann + the Richmonds, 8 people) IS THE WRONG FAMILY WE DELIBERATELY CUT OFF. It is disconnected BY DESIGN. Re-attaching it would undo the untangle. Section 6 is not a to-do list; check whether an island was severed on purpose before joining anything

blocked.name: Henry Whitehead
tried: His HINTS hold no death - only a family tree, and an obituary for a 'Sir Henry Whitehead' of Bradford who died abt 1928 at 'Siagenhoe Park'. Different man; do not attach

death index 1916-2007 (coll 7579) name + birth 1859 +/-2 AND +/-5 - both returned the SAME 7 rows, so that birth filter is not binding. A frozen result set that ignores a widened range is a broken filter, not an absence
coll 7579 + death place = Durham (he was at Sunderland in 1911) - 50 rows, no Henry Whitehead of the right age died in Sunderland or anywhere in Durham

coll 7579 + death place = Yorkshire West Riding, scanned for Thorne/Doncaster/Goole and for the b.1855-64 age bracket - nothing at Thorne. A Henry Whitehead abt 1859 died Sheffield Q4 1923, but Sheffield is nowhere in his life

⭐ THE DEFINITIVE ENGLISH SWEEP, DONE ON GRO - every death registered in England and Wales 1911-1957 (he is alive at the 1911 census, so 1911 is a hard floor), surname WHITEHEAD exact, forename Henry WITH derivative variations so HARRY is included, male: 434 rows, age read off every one. 39 men compute to b.1855-1863 and NOT ONE dies in his geography - nothing at Doncaster, Thorne, Goole, Grimsby, Sunderland, Durham or York. English deaths are now COVERED AND EMPTY; do not re-run them

The only four northern hits, none of them attachable: Sheffield 1918 M (b.1863, age 55) / SHEFFIELD 1923 D vol 09C p.490 (b.1859, age 64 - a PERFECT age fit and still rejected, because Sheffield appears nowhere in his life and an age match on a name this common proves nothing) / Leeds North 1938 J HARRY (b.1862) / Worksop 1946 D (b.1859)

NOT COVERED, and I would rather say so - GRO TIMES OUT on sound-alike surname variants (>45s, wedges the evaluate channel), so a badly mangled WHITEHEAD would have slipped the sweep. Get variant spellings from Ancestry and re-test those few by hand
blocked.found_instead: The reason earlier searches failed: HE LEFT YORKSHIRE. His residences run Beverley 1861 -> York 1871 -> Cottingham 1881 -> Quebec 1886 -> York 1891 and 1901 -> SUNDERLAND, Co. Durham 1911 -> and an undated GREAT GRIMSBY, Lincolnshire. Meanwhile his wife ADA died Jan 1927 at THORNE, Yorkshire West Riding - so the family came back south at some point.
next: ⭐ THE REAL PATTERN - THREE WHITEHEADS ARE ALL MISSING FROM 1921: Henry, his wife ADA, and their son ALFRED (see Alfred's own entry - 65 candidates searched, none right). Three absences across two generations is not three coincidences, it is ONE cause. Stop hunting them individually and find the cause: were they abroad in June 1921 (Henry had already emigrated to Quebec once, in 1886), or is the household mis-indexed under a mangled surname? Alice's 1921 shows her at her MOTHER's house without Alfred, which fits a family scattered or away

1921 SEARCHES TRIED AND FAILED: name+birth 1859 +/-3 (no place) and name+birth+Beverley BOTH returned the SAME 7 rows - the 1921 collection's birth filter FREEZES the result set exactly like the death index does. A frozen count is a broken filter, not an absence. Also tried residence=Great Grimsby (50 rows, no Ada) and Ada+birthplace=Wetwang (filter did not bind)
ANCHOR ON ADA. She died Jan 1927 at Thorne (Doncaster area). Find HER death record and check whether she is described as a WIDOW - that alone brackets Henry's death before or after Jan 1927

⛔ STOP SEARCHING ENGLISH DEATHS. The GRO sweep above covered the whole country for 46 years. Two absences now point the same way - missing from the 1921 census AND missing from the national death register - and GRO covers England and Wales ONLY. He crossed to Quebec once already, in 1886. Look at SCOTTISH (ScotlandsPeople), IRISH and CANADIAN records, and at deaths at sea
GRO caveat for whoever runs it next - its Age field is BROKEN and returns zero rows even for ages that plainly exist. Leave Age blank, pull every row, and filter the age yourself in script

blocked.name: Ann Emms - almost certainly Ann POTTLE
tried: Norfolk BT transcripts (coll 61636) for a marriage - the record already on her is NOT a marriage, it is son John Robert's baptism. Found a second baptism the same way (son William, BT ANF 1846)
coll 61636 with a marriage-year filter - returned ZERO; that filter is too tight, drop it and scan
blocked.found_instead: SOLVED - IT IS PROVEN. The civil index only gave a 50/50 (page 439 carried one man and two women: Thomas Emms / Ann Pottle / Rachel Sadler, second groom untranscribed). The PARISH REGISTER settles it - found in Thomas Emms's hints, coll 61043 rec 629479, Norfolk RO PD 66/8: 'Thomas Emms, Full Age, 14 May 1843, NORWICH ST MICHAEL AT PLEA, Father ROBERT EMMS, Spouse ANN POTTLE'. It names BOTH PARTIES. Attached, and the marriage date upgraded from the vague 'April 1843' registration quarter to 14 May 1843. Her baptism (5 Apr 1818 Wymondham) and her parents WILLIAM POTTLE + RHODA MAYES are now on the tree.
next: ⭐ SHE MARRIED TWICE - SHE IS 'ANN WRIGHT' AFTER c.1860. Thomas died Jan 1855; she remarried a WRIGHT and by 1871 is HEAD at York, St John Micklegate (RG10/4747 f.18 p.30) with her sons James Emms 19 and Thomas Emms 18, born Wymondham - so widowed a SECOND time. Attached. HER DEATH WILL BE INDEXED UNDER WRIGHT, not Emms or Pottle - that is why it was never found

TRIED - death index (8914) Ann Wright b.1819 +/-5: 50 rows, none at York in the right window. But the result is CAPPED at 50 and 'Ann Wright' is extremely common. Narrow by York district, and by 1901+ once her 1881/1891 censuses are found

NOT ATTACHED, needs the image - 1901 York (coll 7814 rec 28471228) has an 'Ann Wright, 77, born Windham Norfolk' living with Thomas Emms 46 (Head) and wife Jane. Thomas 46 fits her son exactly. BUT she is entered as MOTHER-IN-LAW of the head, which makes her the wife's mother, not his. Either a loose entry or a different family - read the household off the image before touching it
STILL MISSING for her: 1881 and 1891 censuses (search Ann WRIGHT at York), and her death

DONE - RENAMED to Ann POTTLE, with the name cited to the 14 May 1843 parish register that proves it. Chris's instruction: 'if it's proven you should be doing it.' The gate is the EVIDENCE, not how permanent the button feels - a rename is reversible, and parking a proven fact leaves the tree wrong in the meantime
Thomas and Ann still need deaths and censuses 1861-1891. Norwich St Peter Mancroft in 1851 is the anchor

blocked.name: William Mills (and Sarah Mills I352794569499)
tried: 1861 surname=Mills + residence=Barkby, and again + residence=Barrow upon Soar - BOTH returned the SAME generic 50 Leicestershire Millses. The residence filter does not bind for these Leicestershire districts; a nil result from it means nothing
1861 Sarah Mills + birthplace=Doveridge Derbyshire - filter loosened to all Derbyshire, no Doveridge Sarah among them
1861 William Mills + birthplace=Sussex - found ONE interesting man (below) but could not corroborate him
blocked.found_instead: 1851 (already attached) anchors them: Barkby, Leicestershire, Barrow upon Soar RD. William 37 head b. 'Walverton, Sussex'; Sarah 33 wife; Eliza 8, JOHN 6 (our ancestor), William 4, Maryan 1 month. ALSO: a William Mills aged 45 b. 'Warburton, Sussex' is a live-in SERVANT in the Morton household at Leicester St Martin in 1861 (rec 21643361, coll 8767). Age is 2 years out and the spelling differs, and I could NOT find Sarah heading a household anywhere to corroborate a family split. NOT ATTACHED.
next: STRONG HUNCH WORTH TESTING: 'Walverton' and 'Warburton' are both probably manglings of WARBLETON, SUSSEX, which is a real parish. Search Sussex baptisms/parish registers for a William Mills bapt. abt 1814 at Warbleton - that would settle his origin and give his parents (gen 8)

Find Sarah first, not William. She is the one with the distinctive birthplace (Doveridge, Derbys) and she would have the children with her. Try 1861/1871 on the CHILDREN's names instead - Eliza b.1843, John b.1845, William b.1847, Mary Ann b.1851, all born Leicestershire
Their marriage should be findable ~1841-42 (Eliza b.1843), and a Sussex man marrying a Derbyshire woman in Leicestershire is an unusual enough combination to search on

blocked.name: Fanny Nicholson - really FANNY BURTON
tried: death index (coll 8914) name=Fanny Nicholson, death 1868 +/-5 - ONE hit, a Gateshead woman b.1781. Wrong

death index name=Frances Nicholson, death 1868 +/-10 - only TWO hits in all England across twenty years, which is not credible for this surname. The death-year filter on this collection throttles far too hard; do not trust a small result set from it
blocked.found_instead: SOLVED. She is FRANCES 'FANNY' BURTON, married GEORGE NICHOLSON 28 JUL 1838 AT WETWANG aged 21, father JOHN BURTON (Wetwang parish register, East Riding Archives PE48/8, coll 62141 rec 90061011). She OUTLIVED GEORGE BY TWELVE YEARS: 1891 Stockton on Tees with her son HENRY, 41 (RG12/4050 f.57 p.10); 1901 Stockton on Tees aged 84 b.Cottingham with her daughter SARAH, now Sarah Colley (RG13/4622 f.17 p.25); DIED Q1 1904 STOCKTON aged 82 (GRO 10A/50, the only one in the district in a decade). Marriage, both censuses and the death are ATTACHED. Proof is a HOUSEHOLD match not a name match - the Henry and the Sarah sheltering her in 1891/1901 are both children in the 1851 Wetwang census.
next: 🚨 RETRACTED, SAME DAY - I attached George's 1881 census, read MARITAL STATUS: WIDOWER, and bracketed her death to 1871x1881. SHE WAS ALIVE AND OUTLIVED HIM BY TWELVE YEARS. The 1881 'Widower' is simply FALSE - one man's answer on one night, from a household his wife had left. Every 1871-1881 death sweep I ran was doomed by my own bracket. NO SINGLE CENSUS FIELD ENDS A SEARCH

☠️ CANDIDATE KILLED - 'Francis Nicholson, age 77, Q4 1894, DRIFFIELD, vol 9d p.213' IS A MAN. GRO holds sex, and a volume+page lookup on that exact reference returns it only when Gender=M. Do not revisit it

⭐ FOR CHRIS TO DECIDE - HER SURNAME SHOULD BE BURTON. It is record-proven by the Wetwang register, and the tree's convention is maiden names (Jane Farrar, Ann Pottle, Elizabeth Weatherill). Not renamed on my own initiative because she is a 4th-great-grandmother. Likewise her FATHER, JOHN BURTON, is named in that register and could be added as a new person - also Chris's call

⛔ DO NOT ATTACH the 1871 Woolton (Lancashire) or 1881 Richmond census hints - they are DIFFERENT WOMEN. Wrong birthplaces, and our George was at Wetwang in 1871. Ancestry is conflating several Frances Nicholsons; her real 1871 and 1881 are still unfound and are the remaining gap
Her mother 'Frances Fanny Eldrington' is TREE-HINT ONLY. Not evidence, do not attach

Where to look for the missing 1871/1881: she is somewhere between Wetwang and Teesside, and by 1891 she is with son Henry at Stockton. Work forward from the CHILDREN's households - Henry b.1849 and Sarah b.1846 - not from her own name

blocked.name: John Farrar
tried: 1851 name=John Farrar + birth=1805 +/-5 and +/-10 - 10 hits, none in the Bradford area, none with a wife Hannah. The filter was the problem: his birth year 1805 is ITSELF unsourced, so I was filtering real records out using an invented number
1851 name=Jane Farrar + birth=1832 Wibsey - 7 hits, no Wibsey Jane (and 1832 is unsourced too; the register says she was 31 in May 1868, so abt 1837)
1851 name=Hannah Farrar + birth=1803 Low Moor - ONE hit, a Newcastle woman. Wrong on every axis

1851 name=John Farrar + residence=Bradford, NO birth year - this finally bound properly and returned 50 real Bradford-area hits, 20 of them heads of household. Only one had a wife Hannah: John Farrar 38, b. Heptonstall, Halifax St James - opened it, and the household is a SCHOOL (ten boarders aged 7-12 plus a servant). Not him
blocked.found_instead: MOSTLY SOLVED. The 1830 MARRIAGE REGISTER, read from the image (coll 2253 rec 16131190, Bradford St Peter entry 36, WYAS BDP14 / 40D90/1/3/14): 'John Farrar, of this Parish, BLACKSMITH and Hannah Lightowlers, of this Parish, Spinster, married by Banns 1 February 1830' - BOTH SIGNED WITH A CROSS. Same trade and same church as Jane's 1868 wedding, 38 years apart, so it is provably the same man. AND THE 1871 CENSUS HAS HIM AT HOME: RG10/4476 f.9 p.12 sch.72, All Saints Horton - John Farrar 66 HEAD b.Halifax, Hannah 68 wife, Daniel Farrer 36 son. John went from ONE source to three; Hannah from zero to a complete life (birth, marriage, 1851, 1861, 1871, 1881, death Q4 1882 Bradford aged 81, GRO 09B/119). Hannah's 1881: 34 Berwick St Bradford, 79, WIDOW, ANNUITANT, living with daughter Jane Best.
next: 🚨 RETRACTED - I had written 'LEAD ONLY, NOT EVIDENCE... TESTED AND FAILED... Do not attach' about Hannah's maiden name LIGHTOWLERS. IT IS RECORD-PROVEN, and the record was in collection 2253 all along - the very collection I said I had searched. My query missed it (singular 'Lightowler' against the register's 'Lightowlers'). A FAILED SEARCH IS NOT A DISPROOF; I turned one null result into a prohibition

🚨 RETRACTED - 'a man working away, or living in at a forge' was a theory built on his absence from 1851 and 1861. In 1871 he is HEAD of his own household with wife and son. Do not build character theories on absences
DONE - John Farrar's birth 1805 HALIFAX IS NOW SOURCED by the 1871 census (age 66, born Halifax). The old WARNING note about it being unsourced can come off
STILL MISSING - JOHN'S DEATH. Hannah is 'Widow' in April 1881, which is a LEAD NOT PROOF (George Nicholson was a 'Widower' with a living wife). If right, he died Apr 1871 x Apr 1881 aged 66-75

BEST CIVIL CANDIDATE, NOT ATTACHED - John Farrar age 64, Q1 1873, BRADFORD 09B/133. Three to four years out on an age that is now census-anchored, and Bradford is thick with Farrars. Needs corroboration before it goes anywhere near the tree

SEARCHED AND EMPTY - West Yorkshire burials (coll 2254) 1871-1881: nine John Farrars in the b.1799-1812 band, ALL in the Calder Valley or Wakefield, NONE at Bradford or Horton. Same Bradford coverage hole that blocked Jane Farrar's burial. He also has NO HINTS AT ALL
NEXT ON JOHN - Bradford's own cemetery registers (Undercliffe, Bowling), which coll 2254 does not cover

DONE - the 1851 image was read and the household IS Farrar. Attached to Jane AND to Hannah, so Hannah went from ZERO sources to one. The image corrected two of my index-based guesses: Hannah is 'Mar' (MARRIED, husband absent) NOT a widow, and 'Wettey M Bradford' is 'WIBSEY nr BRADFORD'

DECIDED AND DONE - Chris ruled 'put wibsey'. Hannah's birthplace is now Wibsey, Yorkshire, England (matching the tree's existing spelling so no new unresolved place), cited to the 1851 census. The unsourced 'Low Moor' is gone

SMALL TIDY-UP OUTSTANDING - her birth DATE is still 1803, but the census gives age 47 = abt 1804, and the census is now cited against that fact. Set it to 'abt 1804' when the fact-edit dialog works (it refuses input while screenshots are down). One-year census drift, not a problem, just unfinished

WIBSEY NOW DOUBLY CONFIRMED - the 1861 image (RG9/3330 f.104 p.5) independently gives her birthplace as 'Bradford, Wibsey'. And note where the old wrong value probably came from: LOW MOOR is on the SAME PAGE, as the birthplace of Rachel Phillips two doors up

NEW FACTS needing the Add-fact dialog: Hannah was a CHARWOMAN (1861). Her daughter Jane was a WORSTED WEAVER (1861) - completing stuff weaver 1851 / worsted weaver 1861 / woollen weaver 1871, one trade under three wordings

TIDY-UP - when attaching the 1861 I cited it against JANE's birth, but that image says Jane was born HALIFAX while the tree (and the 1851 image) say WIBSEY. The 1861 INDEX contradicts its own image on this. Either untick that citation or record the variant; do not change Jane's birthplace, which has the 1851 image behind it

TRIED AND FAILED - hunting John in the 1851 census ELSEWHERE (Hannah is 'Mar' with husband absent, so he is enumerated somewhere else that night). Searched coll 8860 name=John Farrar + keyword=blacksmith: the keyword param does NOT bind, returned the same generic 50, and there is no John Farrar lodging or heading a household anywhere in the Bradford/Bowling area. Halifax has several but none tie to Hannah

IMPORTANT - HE WAS STILL ALIVE IN 1861. The 1861 image (RG9/3330 f.104 p.5 sch.26, Little Horton) has Hannah as Head, condition 'Mar' AGAIN, ten years after 1851 - so do NOT bound his death before 1861. He is absent from her household in BOTH censuses, which is a pattern worth explaining rather than a gap: a man working away, or living in at a forge

NEXT ON JOHN - try the trade from the other end: UK City and County Directories (coll 3145) for a FARRAR, blacksmith, Bradford/Bowling/Wibsey 1840s-60s. A blacksmith is a rated, listed tradesman. Also worth a Bradford burial search for John Farrar 1851-1868 in case he died before the daughter's wedding (the register names a father whether alive or dead)
NEXT - read Rachel's surname off the image at higher magnification (cropped the canvas but not enough to be certain). Ancestry read 'Rachel Segltowler'; it is probably LIGHTOWLER

NOW WORTH REVISITING - her parents DANIEL LIGHTOWLERS + MARY BENTLEY are hint-only, but the maiden name that underpinned them is now proven, and her son is named Daniel. Her baptism hint (25 Sep 1803, WIBSEY, father Daniel Lightowlers) matches the birthplace Chris ruled on. Attach the baptism and the parents become a real question for Chris rather than a guess
Search 1851/1861 Bradford on the TRADE: a blacksmith is a rated, named tradesman - try UK City and County Directories (coll 3145) for a Farrar, blacksmith, Bradford, 1840s-60s

Work backwards from Jane instead: she is in the 1871/1881/1891 censuses already attached. Find her in 1851 or 1861 as a daughter at home and the whole household - father, mother, siblings - comes with her in one record

DONE - the 1868 register IS now attached to him (zero sources -> one). Method: open the merge URL, UNTICK EVERY checkbox (the ticked ones were 'Marriage to Jane Farrar' and 'Include Jane Farrar' as a NEW PERSON duplicate), then Save to tree. That attaches the citation alone, changing no facts and creating no people. Verified after reload: relationships still Hannah + Jane only

STILL TO FIX - Ancestry hung that citation on his BIRTH fact, implying the register evidences 1805 Halifax. It does not; it evidences his NAME, his TRADE and that he fathered Jane. Re-point it to the Occupation 1868 fact (needs the edit-citation dialog, so needs screenshots working)

blocked.name: Alfred Whitehead
tried: 65 Alfred Whiteheads b.1884-88 in coll 63150 - none in the right place
spouse=Alice and spouse=Emily - nothing (Emily Mallinson is NOT his wife; that was a misread of the next GEDCOM record)
residence=Leeds exact - returned zero, filter too tight

birthplace=Canada, which should have been decisive since he was born 27 Dec 1886 at York Mills, Toronto - EXACTLY ONE hit in the whole collection and it is a different man (Alfred B Whitehead b.1866, visitor in Westminster)
blocked.found_instead: Alice's 1921 (rec 36648706): she and baby Annie were at her MOTHER's house, 15 Willington Lane, Leeds. Alfred is not in the household - they were living apart by June 1921. That is why every spouse search failed.
next: Search 1921 on his OCCUPATION - 'Joiner' is now recorded on his profile from the 1893 probate
Work backwards from the 1939 Register, where he is already attached in the West Riding, to get an address to search from
Consider that he may simply not be in England in June 1921

blocked.name: Elizabeth Weatherill
blocked.found_instead: SOLVED AND ATTACHED - BURIED 2 MAY 1883 AT SHERIFF HUTTON (coll 62235 rec 529069), death registered Q2 1883 Malton age 77 (GRO 09D/283), plus a NEW 1881 census: Roans Cottage, Sheriff Hutton West Cornbrough, age 74, MONTHLY NURSE, living as mother-in-law with Thomas and Hannah Rowlay (RG11/4814 f.104 p.10). Sheriff Hutton W Cornbrough registered at MALTON, which is why Easingwold and York searches missed her.
next: 🚨 THE OLD BRACKET WAS WRONG AND COST THE EARLIER SEARCHES - I had 'died 1871x1881, because James is alone at Lillings Ambo in 1881'. His 1881 entry says MARITAL STATUS: MARRIED. They simply LIVED APART. She died 1883, outside the window I was searching, so 'none of 55 candidates match' was guaranteed before I started. THIRD time this tree has fooled me this way (also George x Fanny Nicholson, John x Hannah Farrar) - AN ABSENCE FROM A HOUSEHOLD IS NOT A DEATH, read the condition column

AND CHECK THE HINTS FIRST - the burial was sitting in her Hints list with the exact date and parish the whole time, while I swept the national civil index across five spellings on two sites. One page load would have done it

STILL OPEN - her BAPTISM (23 Mar 1806, Sheriff Hutton, father Thomas Webster) is a hint, not attached, and it makes her b.1806 not the 'abt 1807' the tree carries. Her maiden name WEBSTER is hint-only too, though four separate hints agree. Attach the baptism and both are settled

blocked.name: Jane Farrar
blocked.found_instead: DEATH IS NOW SETTLED AND ATTACHED - June 1896, Bradford (civil index 8914 rec 2710469). GRO broke the tie the index could not: its free search gives AGE AT DEATH 63, which the index does not carry. 63 in 1896 = b. 1832/33, matching 20 in 1851 and 29 in 1861. The rival Q4 1891 candidate was b. abt 1828, and GRO shows only ONE Jane Best in Bradford across 1892-96. The old 'do not attach either candidate' caution is DISCHARGED.
next: Her age is still irreconcilable across censuses: 28 in 1871 (b.~1843) against 49 in 1881 (b.~1832). 1851, 1861, the 1868 register and now GRO all support ~1832, so 1871 is the outlier - treat it as a transcription/enumerator error, not a rival birth year
Her baptism at Wibsey would settle the birth outright. That is the remaining gap, not the death

Her BURIAL is still unfound - West Yorkshire burials (coll 2254) has no Bradford coverage for 1896. Try the Bradford cemetery registers, and note her husband James R. Best died Sep 1898, so the same ground likely holds both

blocked.name: James R. Best
tried: 1851 exact birthplace Tadcaster - zero
1851 loosened + Yorkshire residence - 52 results, residence filter did not bind, no Tadcaster-born James Best
1851 EVERY Best resident in Tadcaster - only 12 people, no James and no Thomas Best household at all
1861 same approaches - 46 results, none Tadcaster-born
1861 with surname dropped entirely - 191,347 results; without a surname the birth filter stops binding, so the drop-the-surname trick does NOT work here
blocked.found_instead: He is recorded as ROBERT in 1871 and JAMES R. in 1881 - James ROBERT Best, who went by Robert. Also: BEST indexes as BERT (a whole Matthew Bert household sits among the Tadcaster Bests).
next: The 1868 Bradford Cathedral marriage register IMAGE - it should give his father Thomas Best's occupation and residence
His father Thomas Best I352793764615 is a blank shell with ZERO records; without his household there is no way to recognise James as a child in 1841/1851. Thomas is the real blocker.
Try searching as ROBERT Best, not James - that is the name the 1871 enumerator used

blocked.name: Henry Bausor
tried: 1841 Southwell household indexed as BARRSON (HO107/867 bk 12 f.16 p.13) - Henry 45, Anne 45, John 15, Martha 10, George 2. READ THE IMAGE: the ages really are 15 and 10, not 18 and 20.
Searched every George Bausor baptism in Notts 1813-1922 - only 8 exist, the sole Southwell one is 1861 to JOHN & Anne, so no George to Henry & Ann
blocked.found_instead: REJECTED as a different Henry & Ann Bausor. Our Martha was a MINOR marrying in 1843 and 28 in 1851 (b.~1823); a Martha aged 10 in 1841 would have been twelve at that wedding. Southwell is a Bausor village - there is also a Thomas Bausor household, a Paul Bauser, and John Bausor's family baptising there for twenty years.
next: Ann was at Lambley in 1851 as 'mother-in-law', which reads like a widow - so Henry probably died 1841x1851, but a death in that window would not tell us WHICH Henry
JOHN CAMPION, who witnessed the 1818 marriage with his mark, is the live lead upward - on a bride's side a same-surname witness is nearly always her father or brother

blocked.name: Henry Sturdy Whitehead
tried: East Riding baptisms (coll 62140) for Henry Whitehead b.1814 Holme upon Spalding Moor - 25 results, no match
same collection searching the given name 'Henry Sturdy' - 38 results, no Sturdy anywhere
York marriages (62233) for a Sturdy bride marrying a Whitehead - 396 results, spouse filter did not bind
blocked.found_instead: The 1844 Howden marriage register (coll 62141, rec 105674426, PE121/21) names his father as WILLIAM WHITEHEAD, INNKEEPER, and Henry himself as a GENTLEMAN'S SERVANT of Howden. It does NOT name his mother - an 1837-form register names fathers only - so the STURDY-as-maiden-name theory is untouched. But his father is no longer a blank name.
next: STRONG NEW ANCHOR: hunt a WILLIAM WHITEHEAD, INNKEEPER, at HOWDEN marrying c.1810-14. An innkeeper is licensed, rated and listed in trade directories - he leaves far more paper than a labourer, and that is a much better search than the blind East Riding baptism sweep that already failed.
Alehouse recognizances / victuallers' licences for Howden, and any Howden trade directory of the 1820s-40s, should name the inn and place the family
If the marriage is found it should name the bride - and the test of the whole theory is whether her surname is STURDY

blocked.name: William Whitehead
tried: WHICH INN DID HE KEEP? Not found. The 1844 Howden marriage register gives his rank as 'Innkeeper' and stops there - marriage registers name the trade, never the premises.

1841 census, every WHITEHEAD resident in Howden - only THREE exist (indexed as WHITCHEAD): John abt 1821, Hannah abt 1811, Ellen abt 1840. NO WILLIAM. So he had probably died before 1841, or was living outside Howden.
UK City and County Directories (coll 3145), Whitehead + Howden exact - ZERO. With name+residence unfiltered it returns 2,835 Leeds hits, so the residence filter is not binding in that collection.
NOTE: the 1844 register did NOT annotate him 'deceased' - but many registers omit that, so it is NOT evidence he was alive in 1844.
blocked.found_instead: *** SOLVED: HE KEPT THE 'HARE AND HOUNDS' AT HOLME UPON SPALDING MOOR. *** East Riding Archives CalmView catalogue, Quarter Sessions QDT/2/10 (Alehouse Recognizances, Holme Beacon Division) - FOUR annual recognizances, all the same man: 1822 QDT/2/10/18, 1823 QDT/2/10/61, 1825 QDT/2/10/104, 1826 QDT/2/10/145. Access Open. The 1822 surety is JOHN SMITH of Holme, carrier. THE CLINCHER IS THE PLACE: Henry Sturdy Whitehead was BORN AT HOLME UPON SPALDING MOOR in 1814, so William kept the pub in the very village his son was born in. That is why every Howden search failed - he was never a Howden innkeeper; he was a HOLME innkeeper whose son later moved to Howden (~7 miles). These four dates, 1822-1826, are the FIRST DATES THIS MAN HAS EVER HAD.
next: *** SEARCH HOLME UPON SPALDING MOOR, NOT HOWDEN. *** Everything was looked for in the wrong parish. His marriage c.1810-14 and his burial are most likely at HOLME, and so is HENRY STURDY WHITEHEAD'S BAPTISM (b.1814 Holme) - which is the record that would finally name his MOTHER and settle the 'Sturdy = maiden name' theory. That baptism is now the single most valuable target on this branch.

Check the alehouse recognizances EITHER SIDE of 1822-1826 - the series is annual, so his licence probably starts earlier and ends later. The end date may effectively date his death or his giving up the house. Series is QDT/2/10, Holme Beacon Division, East Riding Archives, and it is catalogued ITEM BY ITEM online and free.
JOHN SMITH of Holme, CARRIER - his 1822 surety. Sureties are usually neighbours, kin or fellow tradesmen; worth a look for a family connection.

Order copies of the four recognizances from East Riding Archives (AccessStatus: Open) - the documents themselves may give more than the catalogue title, and would be a lovely thing to have for a story.

METHOD NOTE FOR THE WHOLE PROJECT: this was found in a FREE PUBLIC LOCAL-AUTHORITY ARCHIVE CATALOGUE (calmview.eastriding.gov.uk) after three Ancestry collections failed. When a trade is known, search the county archive catalogue EARLY - Quarter Sessions papers list tradesmen by name and often name their premises.

blocked.name: William Laverack
blocked.found_instead: Also a zero-record shell until the same 1844 register gave him GAMEKEEPER, 1844. His daughter Martha was baptised 25 Jan 1818 at Snaith (Cowick), and the tree carries a wife, Ann Wright, whose maiden name came off that baptism transcription.
next: A gamekeeper works for a named estate - the Howden/Cowick area landowner's records may place him
William x Ann Wright's own marriage, c.1810s, Snaith/Cowick - it would also cross-check the Wright surname, which currently rests only on a baptism transcription

blocked.name: Thomas LACKEY
tried: 1891 Thomas Lackey b.1863 Birkenhead exact - 19 results, none in Yorkshire
1891 every LACKEY resident in Hunslet - only 8 people, no Thomas
1891 every Lackey born BIRKENHEAD in the whole of England - only FOUR exist, and neither Hunslet one is him
1891 Thomas Lackey across the Leeds district - 123 results, all Lakey/Lockey of the wrong age and birthplace
1891 on the IRISH reading (after the birthplace work below): every Thomas Lackey born IRELAND in the whole census - only FOUR, none in Yorkshire

1891 SURNAME DROPPED ENTIRELY - every Thomas born IRELAND resident in HUNSLET - 44 people, and none of them is him. Checked the one tempting hit, 'Tom Ewes, 30, b. BELFAST, Boarder' (rec 5775315): he boards with Robert Paul's family, no Emms and no Lackey connection, age 30 against Thomas's 27, and 'Ewes' is not a plausible mangling of 'Lackey'. REJECTED.
So BOTH readings of his birthplace have now been searched and BOTH fail. He is not findable in 1891 as a Birkenhead-born or an Ireland-born Thomas Lackey, nor as any Irish-born Thomas in Hunslet.

THE BRIDE'S HOUSEHOLD - also checked, also negative for him. Sarah Jane Emms IS in 1891 (rec 4027244): aged 20, BOARDER with her sister Elizabeth Emms 19, in Charles E Taylor's house at Hunslet, RG12/3665 folio 24 page 41, ED 35. Thomas is not in that household. Note it is the SAME PIECE (RG12/3665) as the Lackey boarding household but a DIFFERENT enumeration district (ED 37, folio 56) - same corner of Hunslet, not the same street.
blocked.found_instead: A cluster of Lackeys boarding together in Hunslet in Mary H Farrar's household (rec 4833266): JAMES Lackey 40 (b.1851 IRELAND), JOHNNY Lackey 27 (b.1864 Birkenhead), WILLIAM Lackey 13 (b.1878 Hunslet). A second household holds William Lackey 35 (b.1855 Birkenhead) and Mary A Lackey 15. Thomas's parents William Lackey and Mary Anne Milligan were BOTH DEAD by 1882, so orphaned siblings boarding together fits.
next: !! HIS BIRTHPLACE IS IN DOUBT, AND IT MAY BE WHY THE 1891 SEARCH FAILED. The 1921 census gives his birth place as BELFAST, ANTRIM, IRELAND. The tree says 27 Jun 1863 BIRKENHEAD. This family IS Irish - his parents are the Irish line, and a James Lackey b.1851 IRELAND boards with the family in 1891. Birkenhead is exactly the port you land at from Belfast. I searched every Lackey born BIRKENHEAD in the whole 1891 census (only four exist, none him) - if he is indexed as Irish-born, that search was aimed at the wrong birthplace. COMPARE THE BIRTHPLACE ACROSS ALL HIS CENSUSES (1871, 1881, 1901, 1911, 1921) BEFORE CHANGING ANYTHING, then re-run 1891 on the winner.

RESOLVED - the 'Johnny Lackey' candidate is his BROTHER. Thomas's profile carries a brother John Lackey b. abt 1864 Birkenhead, so 'Johnny Lackey, 27, b.1864 Birkenhead', the boarder in the 1891 Hunslet household, is John and not Thomas. Do not attach it to Thomas.
He married in OCTOBER 1891 at Hunslet, six months after the census, so in April he was single and probably boarding - check the household his bride Sarah Jane Emms was in
Work out his siblings first; the boarding cluster is probably the family

blocked.name: WILLIAM SAWDON
tried: coll 62230 (York CoE Baptisms/Marriages/Burials 1538-1812) for a William Sawdon baptism c.1745 Weaverthorpe - the date filter does not bind and every INDEXED Weaverthorpe Sawdon baptism it returns starts in the 1780s (1784, 1786, 1787, 1789, 1797, 1811, 1812). The 1730s-50s stretch of that register is barely indexed - normal for pre-1754.

One 1740s entry did surface, almost by accident: Rebekah Sawdon, bapt 2 Nov 1744 Weaverthorpe, father Wm Sawdon (rec 610198, Borthwick PR/WEA/1). So a Wm Sawdon WAS fathering children at Weaverthorpe in the 1740s - but that record is about Rebekah, not about our William, and proves nothing on its own.
blocked.found_instead: FATHER SOLVED - it was in a record ALREADY ATTACHED to him and never opened: William Sawden, bapt 1737 Weaverthorpe, FATHER WILLIAM SAWDEN (Borthwick PR/WEA/1, coll 62230 rec 610044). The parent link had simply never been made. Father now on the tree as a new gen 10. Two things this exposed, BOTH STILL OPEN: (1) his birth is recorded as 1745 but he was BAPTISED 1737 - impossible, and the 1745 is the one resting on Geneanet/tree sources while 1737 comes from the register, so he was probably born c.1737; (2) his profile DISPLAYS the baptism as '8 May 1737 North Burton, York' but the attached register entry says WEAVERTHORPE - the fact carries two sources that disagree on the parish, and the derivative index looks to be the one that is wrong.
next: FIX THE BIRTH YEAR: 1745 cannot stand against a 1737 baptism. A register beats a derivative index. This is a deliberate fact edit on a direct ancestor, so it needs doing on purpose rather than as a side effect.
FIX OR SPLIT THE BAPTISM PLACE: North Burton vs Weaverthorpe. Read the image at coll 62230 rec 610044 and let the register decide.

MOTHER: adding the father surfaced an Ancestry hint 'Francis Tindall - Potential mother', and one of the floating Geneanet imports in the tree is 'Frances Tindale b.1690 Weaverthorpe'. The hint and the junk import AGREE, which proves nothing - they are probably the same strangers' tree feeding both. DO NOT LINK HER. Prove it instead from a William Sawden x [bride] marriage at Weaverthorpe before 1737.

The 7 floating Sawdons baptised Weaverthorpe 1737-1756 are sourced ONLY to Geneanet, not to any register, and the register has Rebekah 1744 where the import has Ann 1744. Do not link them on a name-and-date match - prove them from PR/WEA/1 or leave them.

blocked.name: William Sawden (the elder, gen 10)
tried: coll 62230 for a William Sawden marriage c.1723-1743 - only TWO turned up in the whole collection, and neither is at Weaverthorpe
blocked.found_instead: CANDIDATE, NOT ATTACHED: William Sawden x SARAH BELL, 14 Oct 1732, WHARRAM PERCY (Borthwick PR/WP/1, coll 62230 rec 1371656). Right name, five years before the 1737 baptism, and Wharram Percy is about 4 miles from Weaverthorpe - but it is a DIFFERENT PARISH and nothing ties this William to the Weaverthorpe one. The rival, William Sawdon x Ann, 2 Jun 1752 Appleton le Street, is too late for a son baptised 1737 and is rejected.
next: !! SARAH BELL (from a parish register) CONTRADICTS Ancestry's hint of 'Francis Tindall - potential mother', which merely agrees with a Geneanet import already floating in problems.txt §6 (Frances Tindale b.1690 Weaverthorpe). Two copies of the same unsourced tree are not corroboration. DO NOT LINK TINDALL.

To settle it: find other children baptised to William Sawden at Weaverthorpe in the 1730s-40s where the register names a MOTHER. Note the 1737 baptism names only the father - pre-1813 registers usually do - so the marriage may be the only route to her.

Borthwick PR/WEA/1 (Weaverthorpe) needs reading by eye for the 1720s-40s; the index does not reach it. Rebekah Sawdon bapt 2 Nov 1744 father Wm (rec 610198) is in that register and is probably a daughter of the same man.

blocked.name: Edith Champion
blocked.found_instead: DELIBERATELY LEFT UNLINKED - we do not know, and Chris's call is to leave her that way. Her mother Annie Discombe married TWICE: William Mayne 12 Oct 1884 Leeds (he DIED March 1888), then Charlie Champion 3 Feb 1889 Buslingthorpe. Edith was born abt 1888, which falls in the ELEVEN-MONTH GAP between the two. So she is either William Mayne's posthumous child or Charlie Champion's born before the wedding. Her four siblings are settled - Emily Mayne (b.1887) to Mayne, Willie/Jessie/Walter to Champion - she is the only one that cannot be placed by date.
next: ONLY her birth registration settles it - exact date, and whether a father is named. Do not assign one on the surname: 'recorded as Champion' is a surname on a tree, not evidence, and a birth a few months before a marriage is a pattern this family already supplies once (Arthur Ingleby).

If she was born Sep-Dec 1888 she was conceived before William Mayne's March 1888 death, which would favour Mayne; a birth early in 1888 favours the same. A late-1888 conception would favour Champion. The registration quarter is therefore the whole question.

death sweep 1901-1911 - nothing
blocked.found_instead: There is probably no marriage to find - Thomas and Emma may never have married, which is consistent with Arthur's birth registration.
next: Treat the maiden name as the target, not the marriage. Arthur's birth registration or his own 1913 marriage lines may name her.
